Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has given me a loan application process with strict normative rules and some recommended best practices I need to analyze three cases from an event log to see where they violate the rules or fall short of recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I should clearly separate the normative violations which are serious from the desiderata issues which are just recommended but not required The normative rules are absolute skipping steps wrong order or doing forbidden actions like finalizing documentation after rejection are all big no nos The desiderata are about timing and optional steps like follow up calls which are not mandatory

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at Case 1 Sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case This follows the exact normative sequence perfectly All steps are in order no skips no forbidden actions For desiderata Credit check was within 24 hours same day but inform applicant was 2 hours after decision well within 48 hours No follow up call mentioned but since it is not required that is fine So Case 1 is clean

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 is messy First issue Risk Assessment at 820am but Credit Check only at 900am That violates the normative rule that Credit Check must come before Risk Assessment Big problem Then Loan Decision Rejected at 930am which is fine since it is after Risk Assessment even though Risk Assessment was out of order But then Finalize Documentation at 1000am after rejection That is explicitly forbidden by normative rules Also closing case at 1030am without following the approved path no finalization needed for rejection

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence looks correct Application Credit Check but 2 days late which is just desiderata Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant 2 days late Finalize Documentation Close Case Normative violations None all steps in perfect order no forbidden actions Desiderata issues Credit check took 53 hours violates within 24 hours recommendation Informing applicant took 51 hours violates within 48 hours recommendation No follow up call before closing violates the recommended best practice

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: I should structure my response clearly First list normative violations per case with specific rule breaks Then list desiderata misses per case For each explain why it matters and whether it is serious

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Case 2 the Risk Assessment before Credit Check is a clear sequence violation and Finalize Documentation after rejection is a direct forbidden action Both are severe normative breaks For Case 3 all the timing issues are just suboptimal but not rule breaking

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The task asks to suggest interpretations or justifications where possible For Case 3 timing delays maybe system issues or workload

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The normative rules do not care about reasons the violations are still violations regardless of why they happened So I should state that while timing delays are suboptimal they do not violate hard rules
